title: Multivessel CAD - Coronary Artery Bypass Grafting
chief_complaint: 65-year-old diabetic male with progressive exertional angina for 6 months. Coronary angiography shows severe triple vessel disease (LAD 90%, LCx 80%, RCA 85%). LVEF 45%. Not a candidate for PCI due to SYNTAX score 35.

hidden_data.angiography: SYNTAX score 35, three-vessel disease, distal targets adequate for grafting

correct_diagnosis: Severe Triple Vessel Coronary Artery Disease - CABG Indicated
key_learning_points: CABG preferred over PCI for: triple vessel + diabetes, SYNTAX >33, LV dysfunction
LIMA to LAD = gold standard graft
Saphenous vein grafts for other vessels
Pre-op: carotid doppler, HbA1c optimization, PFT
Post-op: DAPT, statin, beta-blocker, cardiac rehab

patient_responses.angina: Chest pain with walking 2 blocks, relieved by rest
patient_responses.medical: I have diabetes and high cholesterol

feedback.correct_message: Excellent! CABG is the right choice for this patient.
feedback.incorrect_message: This patient needs CABG. The triple vessel disease + diabetes + SYNTAX >33 are indications.
feedback.attending_note: CABG: LIMA to LAD + saphenous vein grafts to LCx and RCA. On-pump vs off-pump based on surgeon preference. Pre-op aspirin continues, stop clopidogrel 5 days before. Post-op: aspirin + statin + beta-blocker + cardiac rehab. Excellent long-term outcomes.
